front: What are the two primary ways to create a thread in Java?
back: 1. Extending the Thread class. 2. Implementing the Runnable interface.

front: What is the main difference between extending Thread and implementing Runnable?
back: Extending Thread means your class cannot extend another class (due to single inheritance), while implementing Runnable allows your class to still extend another class.

front: What method must you override when extending the Thread class?
back: The run() method. This method contains the code that will be executed when the thread is started.

front: How do you start the execution of a thread?
back: By calling the start() method on the Thread object. This method internally calls the run() method on a new thread of execution.

front: Why is it incorrect to call the run() method directly instead of start()?
back: Calling run() directly executes the code in the current thread, like a normal method call. It does not create a new thread of execution, defeating the purpose of multithreading.
